{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Executive",
  "description": "Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Executive

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Customer Service Manager

Job Summary


	The Customer Service Executive will be responsible for managing customer interactions, ensuring timely and accurate order processing, and maintaining excellent service standards.
	The role requires close collaboration with the sales team to meet customer expectations and achieve organizational goals.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist customers in creating new orders, tracking deliveries, and making changes to existing orders.
	Work closely with internal team members (Salesmen) to ensure all customer needs are effectively met.
	Prepare and present weekly sales reports comparing actual sales against projected targets.
	Generate quotations and invoices for customer orders in a timely and accurate manner.
	Maintain detailed and accurate records of customer complaints, feedback, and resolutions.
	Build and nurture strong relationships with customers to enhance satisfaction and retention.
	Support the Customer Service Manager in daily operations and administrative tasks as required.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in any relevant field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in a customer service or client-facing role.
	Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel - Pivot Table, V-LOOKUP).
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.
	Ability to multitask, prioritize responsibilities, and work effectively in a fast-paced environment.


Salary&nbsp;
N150,000 / Month.

Method of Applictaion&nbsp;
Interested and qualify candidate should send CVs to: cv@ascentech.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Service Executive Job at Ascentech Services Limited




        
    

        
        Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Executive

Job Location: Ilupeju, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Customer Service Manager

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Customer Service Executive will be responsible for managing customer interactions, ensuring timely and accurate order processing, and maintaining excellent service standards.
	The role requires close collaboration with the sales team to meet customer expectations and achieve organizational goals.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist customers in creating new orders, tracking deliveries, and making changes to existing orders.
	Work closely with internal team members (Salesmen) to ensure all customer needs are effectively met.
	Prepare and present weekly sales reports comparing actual sales against projected targets.
	Generate quotations and invoices for customer orders in a timely and accurate manner.
	Maintain detailed and accurate records of customer complaints, feedback, and resolutions.
	Build and nurture strong relationships with customers to enhance satisfaction and retention.
	Support the Customer Service Manager in daily operations and administrative tasks as required.


Requirements and Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in any relevant field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in a customer service or client-facing role.
	Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel - Pivot Table, V-LOOKUP).
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.
	Ability to multitask, prioritize responsibilities, and work effectively in a fast-paced environment.


Salary 
N150,000 / Month.

Method of Applictaion 
Interested and qualify candidate should send CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Audit Manager (Manufacturing)",
  "description": "Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Audit Manager (Manufacturing)

Job Location: Ogun (with travel to Kano and Cameroon)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	We are seeking an experienced Internal Audit Manager to support governance, risk management, and internal control across our multi-site manufacturing operations in Nigeria and Cameroon.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute risk-based internal audit plans across 3 Ogun plants, Kano, and Cameroon operations
	Review manufacturing processes including inventory, production costing, procurement, and fixed assets
	Conduct surprise stock counts and verify raw materials, WIP, and finished goods
	Assess operational, financial, tax, and regulatory compliance risks
	Identify control gaps, inefficiencies, and fraud risks using data analytics
	Prepare audit reports and track resolution of findings to closure
	Work closely with plant and finance teams to strengthen internal controls


Requirements


	ICAN qualification is mandatory (ACCA acceptable); CIA/CISA is an advantage
	4&ndash;7 years of audit experience, with strong exposure to manufacturing (FMCG/industrial preferred)
	Strong understanding of costing, inventory management, and production processes
	Experience with ERP systems such as SAP or Microsoft Dynamics
	Willingness to travel frequently within Nigeria and internationally (Cameroon)
	Big 4 experience is an added advantage.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: cv@ascentech.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-31",

Internal Audit Manager (Manufacturing) Job at Ascentech Services Limited




        
    

        
        Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Audit Manager (Manufacturing)

Job Location: Ogun (with travel to Kano and Cameroon)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced Internal Audit Manager to support governance, risk management, and internal control across our multi-site manufacturing operations in Nigeria and Cameroon.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute risk-based internal audit plans across 3 Ogun plants, Kano, and Cameroon operations
	Review manufacturing processes including inventory, production costing, procurement, and fixed assets
	Conduct surprise stock counts and verify raw materials, WIP, and finished goods
	Assess operational, financial, tax, and regulatory compliance risks
	Identify control gaps, inefficiencies, and fraud risks using data analytics
	Prepare audit reports and track resolution of findings to closure
	Work closely with plant and finance teams to strengthen internal controls


Requirements


	ICAN qualification is mandatory (ACCA acceptable); CIA/CISA is an advantage
	4–7 years of audit experience, with strong exposure to manufacturing (FMCG/industrial preferred)
	Strong understanding of costing, inventory management, and production processes
	Experience with ERP systems such as SAP or Microsoft Dynamics
	Willingness to travel frequently within Nigeria and internationally (Cameroon)
	Big 4 experience is an added advantage.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Key Responsibilities


	Manage and monitor patients throughout the dialysis process from admission to discharge
	Deliver safe, effective, and individualized patient care
	Work closely with the Head Nurse and medical team to handle clinical procedures and emergencies
	Ensure accurate patient scheduling and smooth daily operations

	Support training and onboarding of new nursing staff
	Assist in outreach activities to increase awareness of the dialysis centre
	Adhere to all clinical protocols, safety standards, and regulatory requirements

	Strong hands-on experience in dialysis procedures and patient care.

N200,000 - N250,000 per month.&nbsp;

Dialysis Nurse Job at Ascentech Services Limited




        
    

        
        Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Dialysis Nurse
Job Location: Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Dialysis Nurse will provide high-quality, patient-centered care to individuals undergoing dialysis. The role involves managing the full dialysis process, ensuring patient safety, and collaborating with the medical team to deliver excellent clinical outcomes.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage and monitor patients throughout the dialysis process from admission to discharge
	Deliver safe, effective, and individualized patient care
	Work closely with the Head Nurse and medical team to handle clinical procedures and emergencies
	Ensure accurate patient scheduling and smooth daily operations
	Maintain and update patient records using the hospital’s IT systems
	Support training and onboarding of new nursing staff
	Assist in outreach activities to increase awareness of the dialysis centre
	Adhere to all clinical protocols, safety standards, and regulatory requirements
	Perform additional duties as assigned by management


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Nursing is compulsory
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in dialysis or related specialty
	Registered Nurse (RN) with the Nursing & Midwifery Council
	Strong hands-on experience in dialysis procedures and patient care.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month. 

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Product Manager",
  "description": "Busha is one of Africa&rsquo;s leading digital asset platforms. We are on a mission to onboard millions of Africans into the crypto economy, and we are building software and services that will enable our users to experience the blockchain-enabled future of finance. Our customers are at the centre of everything we do, and we are obsessed with creating a pleasant experience throughout their entire crypto journey.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Product Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Overview


	We are looking for a highly driven and execution-oriented Product Manager to join our growing Product team.
	This role is ideal for someone who combines strong product thinking with operational execution and is passionate about building user-focused financial technology products at scale.
	As a Product Manager at Busha, you will work closely with Engineering, Design, Operations, Compliance, Growth, and Leadership teams to drive product strategy, define requirements, manage product delivery, and improve customer experience across our platforms and services.
	You will be expected to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution in a fast-paced and evolving environment.


Responsibilities


	Own and manage product initiatives from ideation through execution and launch
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define product requirements and priorities
	Translate business goals and customer needs into clear product roadmaps and user stories
	Drive product delivery and coordinate execution across Engineering, Design, and Operations teams
	Conduct product research, competitive analysis, and customer insight gathering
	Monitor product performance, user behavior, and operational metrics to inform decision-making
	Work closely with Compliance and Operations teams to ensure regulatory and operational alignment
	Support process improvements and operational scalability across products
	Prioritize features and initiatives based on business impact, customer value, and technical feasibility
	Communicate product updates, timelines, and priorities effectively to stakeholders
	Contribute to improving overall product development processes and product culture within the organization
	Assess and address technical risks.
	Facilitate the production and maintenance of proper product documentation


The ideal candidate must have:


	3 - 6 years of experience in Product Management, Product Operations, Business Analysis, or related roles
	Strong background in software engineering as well as prior experience in managing a product throughout its lifecycle
	Experience working in fintech, crypto, payments, financial services, or high-growth technology environments is strongly preferred
	Strong understanding of product development lifecycle and agile delivery processes
	Ability to work cross-functionally and manage multiple stakeholders effectively
	Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Experience working closely with engineering and technical teams
	Strong attention to detail and execution capability
	Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced and evolving environment
	Customer-focused mindset with strong product intuition.


Nice to have:


	Experience with crypto/web3 products or digital asset platforms
	Experience using tools such as Jira, Notion, Figma, Linear, or similar platforms
	Technical understanding of APIs, product systems, or infrastructure concepts
	Data analysis or product analytics experience.


What we offer


	Opportunity to work on impactful financial technology products across Africa
	Fast-paced and collaborative work environment
	Career growth and learning opportunities
	Exposure to cross-functional and strategic product work
	Competitive compensation and benefits
	Hybrid work environment.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-01",

Product Manager Job at Busha Digital LTD




        
    

        
        Busha is one of Africa’s leading digital asset platforms. We are on a mission to onboard millions of Africans into the crypto economy, and we are building software and services that will enable our users to experience the blockchain-enabled future of finance. Our customers are at the centre of everything we do, and we are obsessed with creating a pleasant experience throughout their entire crypto journey.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Product Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a highly driven and execution-oriented Product Manager to join our growing Product team.
	This role is ideal for someone who combines strong product thinking with operational execution and is passionate about building user-focused financial technology products at scale.
	As a Product Manager at Busha, you will work closely with Engineering, Design, Operations, Compliance, Growth, and Leadership teams to drive product strategy, define requirements, manage product delivery, and improve customer experience across our platforms and services.
	You will be expected to balance strategic thinking with hands-on execution in a fast-paced and evolving environment.


Responsibilities


	Own and manage product initiatives from ideation through execution and launch
	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define product requirements and priorities
	Translate business goals and customer needs into clear product roadmaps and user stories
	Drive product delivery and coordinate execution across Engineering, Design, and Operations teams
	Conduct product research, competitive analysis, and customer insight gathering
	Monitor product performance, user behavior, and operational metrics to inform decision-making
	Work closely with Compliance and Operations teams to ensure regulatory and operational alignment
	Support process improvements and operational scalability across products
	Prioritize features and initiatives based on business impact, customer value, and technical feasibility
	Communicate product updates, timelines, and priorities effectively to stakeholders
	Contribute to improving overall product development processes and product culture within the organization
	Assess and address technical risks.
	Facilitate the production and maintenance of proper product documentation


The ideal candidate must have:


	3 - 6 years of experience in Product Management, Product Operations, Business Analysis, or related roles
	Strong background in software engineering as well as prior experience in managing a product throughout its lifecycle
	Experience working in fintech, crypto, payments, financial services, or high-growth technology environments is strongly preferred
	Strong understanding of product development lifecycle and agile delivery processes
	Ability to work cross-functionally and manage multiple stakeholders effectively
	Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Experience working closely with engineering and technical teams
	Strong attention to detail and execution capability
	Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced and evolving environment
	Customer-focused mindset with strong product intuition.


Nice to have:


	Experience with crypto/web3 products or digital asset platforms
	Experience using tools such as Jira, Notion, Figma, Linear, or similar platforms
	Technical understanding of APIs, product systems, or infrastructure concepts
	Data analysis or product analytics experience.


What we offer


	Opportunity to work on impactful financial technology products across Africa
	Fast-paced and collaborative work environment
	Career growth and learning opportunities
	Exposure to cross-functional and strategic product work
	Competitive compensation and benefits
	Hybrid work environment.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Personal Assistant to GCEO",
  "description": "Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited was incorporated in December 2009 with the business objective of providing financial service to micro clients who were excluded from the mainstream financial system. It commenced operations in June 2010 upon obtaining a Unit Microfinance Banking License from the Central Bank of Nigeria. It later obtained a State Microfinance Banking License in 2016. Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited&rsquo;s financial inclusion effort focuses on promoting savings culture, access to credit facilities for income-generation purposes and reducing vulnerability to risk through micro insurance.

The Bank&rsquo;s major strategic focus employs the group lending methodology in its operations with a leaning towards women. It believes in the commitment of women folk to work hard and help their families conquer poverty. As such, more than 95% of the Bank&rsquo;s lending activities focuses on women. The success of the bank&rsquo;s group lending activities largely depends on group solidarity and cohesion. The success recorded by the bank in its lending program has earned it strategic partnership with the Bank of Industry (BOI) and the Development Bank of Nigeria (DBN) among others. The combination of cutting-edge technology, a strong and committed Board of Directors and motivated employees has helped the Bank to create a brand widely accepted by its teeming clients in Lagos. It is now expanding its frontiers in a scaling program aimed at delivering improved value to its stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant to GCEO
Job Pocation: Ogba, Lagos

Responsibilities


	Manage and maintain the GCEO&rsquo;s calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel schedules.
	Coordinate internal and external meetings, including preparing agendas, minutes, and follow-up actions.
	Screen phone calls, emails, and correspondence on behalf of the GCEO.

Personal Assistant to GCEO Job at Bosak Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited was incorporated in December 2009 with the business objective of providing financial service to micro clients who were excluded from the mainstream financial system. It commenced operations in June 2010 upon obtaining a Unit Microfinance Banking License from the Central Bank of Nigeria. It later obtained a State Microfinance Banking License in 2016. Bosak Microfinance Bank Limited’s financial inclusion effort focuses on promoting savings culture, access to credit facilities for income-generation purposes and reducing vulnerability to risk through micro insurance.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



The Bank’s major strategic focus employs the group lending methodology in its operations with a leaning towards women. It believes in the commitment of women folk to work hard and help their families conquer poverty. As such, more than 95% of the Bank’s lending activities focuses on women. The success of the bank’s group lending activities largely depends on group solidarity and cohesion. The success recorded by the bank in its lending program has earned it strategic partnership with the Bank of Industry (BOI) and the Development Bank of Nigeria (DBN) among others. The combination of cutting-edge technology, a strong and committed Board of Directors and motivated employees has helped the Bank to create a brand widely accepted by its teeming clients in Lagos. It is now expanding its frontiers in a scaling program aimed at delivering improved value to its stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant to GCEO
Job Pocation: Ogba, Lagos

Responsibilities


	Manage and maintain the GCEO’s calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel schedules.
	Coordinate internal and external meetings, including preparing agendas, minutes, and follow-up actions.
	Screen phone calls, emails, and correspondence on behalf of the GCEO.
	Prepare reports, presentations, briefing notes, and confidential documents.
	Handle sensitive information with a high level of confidentiality and professionalism.
	Liaise with senior executives, board members, clients, government officials, and stakeholders.
	Organize business travel arrangements, accommodation, visas, and itineraries.
	Track deadlines, priorities, and key deliverables for the GCEO.
	Assist in project coordination and monitor execution of strategic initiatives.
	Conduct research and compile data to support executive decision-making.
	Ensure efficient office administration and smooth day-to-day executive operations.
	Attend meetings where required and provide accurate documentation of discussions and action points.
	Manage filing systems, records, and executive correspondence.
	Coordinate special events, executive engagements, and corporate functions.
	Perform other administrative and executive support duties as assigned by the GCEO.
